summary: Greece's activity is led through the Ministry of Digital Governance, whose observatory registry lists the EU-funded PQ-REACT project on a post-quantum cryptography framework and which has promoted Greece's standing in European quantum communications. The posture is one of research and infrastructure participation, with no published national migration guidance, algorithm list or timeline.
govActors: Ministry of Digital Governance | Greek ministry responsible for digital policy and cyber security
provenance: PQ-REACT post-quantum cryptography framework project: Ministry of Digital Governance observatory registry listing (ingested)
European quantum communications standing: Ministry of Digital Governance news item (ingested, references quantum communications/QKD rather than explicit PQC migration) - REVIEW
HellasQCI consultation item flagged not included (consortium, off trusted-domain list) - noted but not used
dominantProcess omitted: no standards camp attested in the documents
No standardFamilies, algorithms, hybridDeployment, migrationTimeline or targetCompletion: none stated in the documents
EU-aligned by membership: participates in the NIS Cooperation Group coordinated PQC roadmap (high-risk by 2030, full by 2035); the timeline shown is the EU coordinated roadmap, not a national one.
Model 2026-06-14 (EU / taker, High): EU coordinated roadmap (NIS Cooperation Group); adopts the NIST baseline as a taker. No national algorithm.

mainRegulation:
NIS2 Directive (EU) 2022/2555, Art. 21(2)(h) | EU | binding-law
DORA, Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 (financial sector) | EU | binding-law
Commission Recommendation (EU) 2024/1101 on a coordinated PQC roadmap | EU | soft-law
NIS Cooperation Group Coordinated Implementation Roadmap (2026/2030/2035) | EU | soft-law
obligation: Essential and important entities must use state-of-the-art cryptography under NIS2, transposed into national law, and financial entities face equivalent duties under DORA; neither yet names post-quantum algorithms specifically. Migration follows the EU coordinated roadmap, with high-risk use cases targeted for 2030 and full migration by 2035, which is encouraged rather than mandated.
